extraction.one_line_summary: DE BONDT won from a four-rider breakaway that misjudged by sprinter teams who panicked too late with gap still 1:11 at 7km to go.
tactical_patterns: Breakaway sandbagged mid-race after early effort, preserving energy while peloton thought they controlled, gaining crucial watts for final 30km.
AFFINI and GABBURO demonstrated domestique-caliber riders executing team tactics within a four-man break rather than protecting individual sprint chances.
Sprinter teams miscalculated effort distribution on Muro di Ca' del Poggio, leaving 1:40 gap with 30km remaining despite multiple teams chasing.

rider_signals.DE BONDT Dries: positive: Won Grand Tour stage by outsprinting stronger-on-paper CORT in four-man tactical breakaway, showing improved finishing ability.
rider_signals.AFFINI Edoardo: positive: Second place demonstrates transition from pure domestique to breakaway finisher capable of contesting against specialists like CORT.
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: neutral: Late mechanical inside 3km raised concerns but received same time ruling, showing vulnerability in critical moments despite no GC loss.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Juan Pedro: negative: Caught in split with 20km remaining on sprinter stage, dropping from 8th to 9th GC, revealing weakness in nervous flat racing.
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: neutral: Won bunch sprint for minor places but team misjudged breakaway threat, showing sprint speed without tactical foresight.
